entities.name: PESTS AND PATHOGENS
entities.description: Organisms including pests and pathogens that can be carried by seeds and vegetative propagules, posing a risk of transboundary spread.

entities.name: GERMPLASM
entities.description: Genetic material of plants, including seeds and vegetative propagules, distributed for agricultural research and conservation.

entities.name: QUARANTINE AND PHYTOSANITARY PROCEDURES
entities.description: Established protocols used by countries to screen export and import consignments to minimize seed-borne pest spread.

entities.name: CGIAR GERMPLASM HEALTH UNITS
entities.description: Multidisciplinary units that ensure phytosanitary protection for the safe conservation and global movement of germplasm.

entities.name: SEED HEALTH TESTING
entities.description: The use of diagnostic tools to detect the presence of pests and pathogens in seed consignments.

entities.name: SEED PHYTOSANITATION
entities.description: The process of treating seeds to eliminate pests and pathogens to ensure safe movement.

entities.name: TRANSBOUNDARY PEST SPREAD
entities.description: The movement of pests and pathogens across international borders via plant propagation materials.

entities.name: CGIAR GENEBANKS AND BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Institutions that acquire and distribute germplasm globally for agricultural research and food security.

entities.name: NATIONAL QUARANTINE SYSTEMS
entities.description: Governmental systems that collaborate with GHUs to regulate the export and distribution of germplasm samples.

entities.name: PHYTOSANITARY REGULATIONS
entities.description: Evolving legal and technical requirements that govern the movement of plant materials to prevent pest spread.

entities.name: PLANT PROPAGATION MATERIALS
entities.description: Materials such as orthodox, intermediate, and recalcitrant seeds and vegetative propagules used for plant reproduction.

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring stable and sufficient food supply, supported by agricultural research and germplasm exchange.
